verses.latin_vulgate: Respondens autem Job, dixit :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיַּ֥עַן אִיּ֗וֹב וַיֹּאמַֽר׃
verses.douay_rheims: But Job answered, and said:
verses.finnish_biblia: Niin Job vastasi ja sanoi:
verses.king_james: But Job answered and said,

verses.latin_vulgate: Utinam appenderentur peccata mea quibus iram merui,  et calamitas quam patior, in statera !
verses.hebrew_masoretic: ל֗וּ שָׁק֣וֹל יִשָּׁקֵ֣ל כַּעְשִׂ֑י (והיתי) [וְ֝הַוָּתִ֗י] בְּֽמֹאזְנַ֥יִם יִשְׂאוּ־יָֽחַד׃
verses.douay_rheims: O that my sins, whereby I have deserved wrath, and the calamity that I suffer, were weighed in a balance.
verses.finnish_biblia: Jos minun surkeuteni punnittaisiin, ja minun kärsimiseni yhtä haavaa laskettaisiin vaa´an päälle,
verses.king_james: Oh that my grief were throughly weighed, and my calamity laid in the balances together!

verses.latin_vulgate: Quasi arena maris hæc gravior appareret ;  unde et verba mea dolore sunt plena :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כִּֽי־עַתָּ֗ה מֵח֣וֹל יַמִּ֣ים יִכְבָּ֑ד עַל־כֵּ֝֗ן דְּבָרַ֥י לָֽעוּ׃
verses.douay_rheims: As the sand of the sea, this would appear heavier: therefore, my words are full of sorrow:
verses.finnish_biblia: Niin se olis raskaampi kuin santa meressä; sentähden ovat minun sanani nielletyt ylös.
verses.king_james: For now it would be heavier than the sand of the sea: therefore my words are swallowed up.

verses.latin_vulgate: quia sagittæ Domini in me sunt,  quarum indignatio ebibit spiritum meum ;  et terrores Domini militant contra me.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כִּ֤י חִצֵּ֪י שַׁדַּ֡י עִמָּדִ֗י אֲשֶׁ֣ר חֲ֭מָתָם שֹׁתָ֣ה רוּחִ֑י בִּעוּתֵ֖י אֱל֣וֹהַּ יַעַרְכֽוּנִי׃
verses.douay_rheims: For the arrows of the Lord are in me, the rage whereof drinketh up my spirit, and the terrors of the Lord war against me.
verses.finnish_biblia: Sillä Kaikkivaltiaan nuolet ovat minussa, joiden myrkky särpää minun henkeni; ja Jumalan kauhistus tarkoittaa minua.
verses.king_james: For the arrows of the Almighty are within me, the poison whereof drinketh up my spirit: the terrors of God do set themselves in array against me.

verses.latin_vulgate: Numquid rugiet onager cum habuerit herbam ?  aut mugiet bos cum ante præsepe plenum steterit ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הֲיִֽנְהַק־פֶּ֥רֶא עֲלֵי־דֶ֑שֶׁא אִ֥ם יִגְעֶה־שּׁ֝֗וֹר עַל־בְּלִילֽוֹ׃
verses.douay_rheims: Will the wild ass bray when he hath grass? or will the ox low when he standeth before a full manger?
verses.finnish_biblia: Huutaako metsä-aasi, kuin hänellä ruohoja on? ammuuko härkä, kuin hänellä on ruokaa?
verses.king_james: Doth the wild ass bray when he hath grass? or loweth the ox over his fodder?

verses.latin_vulgate: aut poterit comedi insulsum, quod non est sale conditum ?  aut potest aliquis gustare quod gustatum affert mortem ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הֲיֵאָכֵ֣ל תָּ֭פֵל מִבְּלִי־מֶ֑לַח אִם־יֶשׁ־טַ֝֗עַם בְּרִ֣יר חַלָּמֽוּת׃
verses.douay_rheims: Or can an unsavoury thing be eaten, that is not seasoned with salt? or can a man taste that which, when tasted, bringeth death?
verses.finnish_biblia: Syödäänkö mautointa ilman suolaa? eli maistaako valkuainen munan ruskuaisen ympäriltä?
verses.king_james: Can that which is unsavoury be eaten without salt? or is there any taste in the white of an egg?

verses.latin_vulgate: Quæ prius nolebat tangere anima mea,  nunc, præ angustia, cibi mei sunt.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: מֵאֲנָ֣ה לִנְגּ֣וֹעַ נַפְשִׁ֑י הֵ֝֗מָּה כִּדְוֵ֥י לַחְמִֽי׃
verses.douay_rheims: The things which before my soul would not touch, now, through anguish, are my meats.
verses.finnish_biblia: Jota minun sieluni ennen kuoitti, se on nyt minun ruokani minun kipuni tähden.
verses.king_james: The things that my soul refused to touch are as my sorrowful meat.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quis det ut veniat petitio mea,  et quod expecto tribuat mihi Deus ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: מִֽי־יִ֭תֵּן תָּב֣וֹא שֶׁאֱלָתִ֑י וְ֝תִקְוָתִ֗י יִתֵּ֥ן אֱלֽוֹהַּ׃
verses.douay_rheims: Who will grant that my request may come: and that God may give me what I look for?
verses.finnish_biblia: Jospa minun rukoukseni tapahtuis, ja Jumala antais minulle, mitä minä toivon!
verses.king_james: Oh that I might have my request; and that God would grant me the thing that I long for!

verses.latin_vulgate: et qui cœpit, ipse me conterat ;  solvat manum suam, et succidat me ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְיֹאֵ֣ל אֱ֭לוֹהַּ וִידַכְּאֵ֑נִי יַתֵּ֥ר יָ֝ד֗וֹ וִיבַצְּעֵֽנִי׃
verses.douay_rheims: And that he that hath begun may destroy me, that he may let loose his hand, and cut me off?
verses.finnish_biblia: Että Jumala tahtois ja löis minun rikki, ja päästäis kätensä särkemään minua;
verses.king_james: Even that it would please God to destroy me; that he would let loose his hand, and cut me off!

verses.latin_vulgate: Et hæc mihi sit consolatio, ut affligens me dolore, non parcat,  nec contradicam sermonibus Sancti.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וּ֥תְהִי־ע֨וֹד ׀ נֶ֘חָ֤מָתִ֗י וַאֲסַלְּדָ֣ה בְ֭חִילָה לֹ֣א יַחְמ֑וֹל כִּי־לֹ֥א כִ֝חַ֗דְתִּי אִמְרֵ֥י קָדֽוֹשׁ׃
verses.douay_rheims: And that this may be my comfort, that afflicting me with sorrow, he spare not, nor I contradict the words of the Holy one.
verses.finnish_biblia: Niin olis minulla vielä sitte lohdutus, ja minä vahvistuisin sairaudessani, ellei hän säästäisi minua: en ole mitään kuitenkaan kieltänyt pyhän puhetta.
verses.king_james: Then should I yet have comfort; yea, I would harden myself in sorrow: let him not spare; for I have not concealed the words of the Holy One.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quæ est enim fortitudo mea, ut sustineam ?  aut quis finis meus, ut patienter agam ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: מַה־כֹּחִ֥י כִֽי־אֲיַחֵ֑ל וּמַה־קִּ֝צִּ֗י כִּֽי־אַאֲרִ֥יךְ נַפְשִֽׁי׃
verses.douay_rheims: For what is my strength, that I can hold out? or what is my end, that I should keep patience?
verses.finnish_biblia: Mikä on minun väkeni, että minä voisin toivoa? ja mikä on minun loppuni, että minun sieluni vois kärsiväinen olla?
verses.king_james: What is my strength, that I should hope? and what is mine end, that I should prolong my life?

verses.latin_vulgate: Nec fortitudo lapidum fortitudo mea,  nec caro mea ænea est.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אִם־כֹּ֣חַ אֲבָנִ֣ים כֹּחִ֑י אִֽם־בְּשָׂרִ֥י נָחֽוּשׁ׃
verses.douay_rheims: My strength is not the strength of stones, nor is my flesh of brass.
verses.finnish_biblia: Onko minun väkeni kivinen? eli minun lihani vaskinen?
verses.king_james: Is my strength the strength of stones? or is my flesh of brass?

verses.latin_vulgate: Ecce non est auxilium mihi in me,  et necessarii quoque mei recesserunt a me.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הַאִ֬ם אֵ֣ין עֶזְרָתִ֣י בִ֑י וְ֝תֻשִׁיָּ֗ה נִדְּחָ֥ה מִמֶּֽנִּי׃
verses.douay_rheims: Behold there is no help for me in myself, and my familiar friends also are departed from me.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ei ole minulla missään apua; ja minun saatuni on pois ajettu minulta.
verses.king_james: Is not my help in me? and is wisdom driven quite from me?

verses.latin_vulgate: Qui tollit ab amico suo misericordiam,  timorem Domini derelinquit.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: לַמָּ֣ס מֵרֵעֵ֣הוּ חָ֑סֶד וְיִרְאַ֖ת שַׁדַּ֣י יַעֲזֽוֹב׃
verses.douay_rheims: He that taketh away mercy from his friend, forsaketh the fear of the Lord.
verses.finnish_biblia: Joka ei osoita lähimmäisellensä laupiutta, hän hylkää Kaikkivaltiaan pelvon.
verses.king_james: To him that is afflicted pity should be shewed from his friend; but he forsaketh the fear of the Almighty.

verses.latin_vulgate: Fratres mei præterierunt me,  sicut torrens qui raptim transit in convallibus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אַ֭חַי בָּגְד֣וּ כְמוֹ־נָ֑חַל כַּאֲפִ֖יק נְחָלִ֣ים יַעֲבֹֽרוּ׃
verses.douay_rheims: My brethren have passed by me, as the torrent that passeth swiftly in the valleys.
verses.finnish_biblia: Minun veljeni ovat rikkoneet minua vastaan, niinkuin oja ja niinkuin väkevä virta ohitse menneet,
verses.king_james: My brethren have dealt deceitfully as a brook, and as the stream of brooks they pass away;

verses.latin_vulgate: Qui timent pruinam,  irruet super eos nix.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הַקֹּדְרִ֥ים מִנִּי־קָ֑רַח עָ֝לֵ֗ימוֹ יִתְעַלֶּם־שָֽׁלֶג׃
verses.douay_rheims: They that fear the hoary frost, the snow shall fall upon them.
verses.finnish_biblia: Jotka ovat kauhiat jäästä, ja jotka lumi peittää.
verses.king_james: Which are blackish by reason of the ice, and wherein the snow is hid:

verses.latin_vulgate: Tempore quo fuerint dissipati, peribunt ;  et ut incaluerit, solventur de loco suo.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: בְּעֵ֣ת יְזֹרְב֣וּ נִצְמָ֑תוּ בְּ֝חֻמּ֗וֹ נִדְעֲכ֥וּ מִמְּקוֹמָֽם׃
verses.douay_rheims: At the time when they shall be scattered they shall perish: and after it groweth hot, they shall be melted out of their place.
verses.finnish_biblia: Kuin helle heitä ahdistaa, pitää heidän nääntymän, ja kuin palava on, pitää heidän katooman paikastansa.
verses.king_james: What time they wax warm, they vanish: when it is hot, they are consumed out of their place.

verses.latin_vulgate: Involutæ sunt semitæ gressuum eorum ;  ambulabunt in vacuum, et peribunt.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: יִ֭לָּ֣פְתוּ אׇרְח֣וֹת דַּרְכָּ֑ם יַעֲל֖וּ בַתֹּ֣הוּ וְיֹאבֵֽדוּ׃
verses.douay_rheims: The paths of their steps are entangled: they shall walk in vain, and shall perish.
verses.finnish_biblia: Heidän polkunsa poikkeevat pois; he raukeevat tyhjään ja hukkuvat.
verses.king_james: The paths of their way are turned aside; they go to nothing, and perish.

verses.latin_vulgate: Considerate semitas Thema, itinera Saba,  et expectate paulisper.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הִ֭בִּיטוּ אׇרְח֣וֹת תֵּמָ֑א הֲלִיכֹ֥ת שְׁ֝בָ֗א קִוּוּ־לָֽמוֹ׃
verses.douay_rheims: Consider the paths of Thema, the ways of Saba, and wait a little while.
verses.finnish_biblia: He katsoivat Temanin tietä, rikkaan Arabian polkuja he toivoivat.
verses.king_james: The troops of Tema looked, the companies of Sheba waited for them.

verses.latin_vulgate: Confusi sunt, quia speravi :  venerunt quoque usque ad me, et pudore cooperti sunt.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: בֹּ֥שׁוּ כִֽי־בָטָ֑ח בָּ֥אוּ עָ֝דֶ֗יהָ וַיֶּחְפָּֽרוּ׃
verses.douay_rheims: They are confounded, because I have hoped: they are come also even unto me, and are covered with shame.
verses.finnish_biblia: Mutta heidän pitää häpiään tuleman, ratki surutoinna ollessansa; ja heidän pitää häpeemän siihen tultuansa.
verses.king_james: They were confounded because they had hoped; they came thither, and were ashamed.

verses.latin_vulgate: Nunc venistis ;  et modo videntes plagam meam, timetis.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כִּֽי־עַ֭תָּה הֱיִ֣יתֶם לֹ֑א תִּֽרְא֥וּ חֲ֝תַ֗ת וַתִּירָֽאוּ׃
verses.douay_rheims: Now you are come: and now, seeing my affliction, you are afraid.
verses.finnish_biblia: Sillä ette nyt mitään ole; ja että te näette surkeuden, pelkäätte te.
verses.king_james: For now ye are nothing; ye see my casting down, and are afraid.

verses.latin_vulgate: Numquid dixi : Afferte mihi,  et de substantia vestra donate mihi ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הֲֽכִי־אָ֭מַרְתִּי הָ֣בוּ לִ֑י וּ֝מִכֹּחֲכֶ֗ם שִׁחֲד֥וּ בַעֲדִֽי׃
verses.douay_rheims: Did I say: Bring to me, and give me of your substance?
verses.finnish_biblia: Olenko minä sanonut: Tuokaat minulle! eli antakaat minun edestäni lahjoja teidän tavarastanne?
verses.king_james: Did I say, Bring unto me? or, Give a reward for me of your substance?

verses.latin_vulgate: vel : Liberate me de manu hostis,  et de manu robustorum eruite me ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וּמַלְּט֥וּנִי מִיַּד־צָ֑ר וּמִיַּ֖ד עָרִיצִ֣ים תִּפְדּֽוּנִי׃
verses.douay_rheims: Or deliver me from the hand of the enemy, and rescue me out of the hand of the mighty?
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja pelastakaat minua vihollisten kädestä, ja vapahtakaat minua tyrannein käsistä?
verses.king_james: Or, Deliver me from the enemy’s hand? or, Redeem me from the hand of the mighty?

verses.latin_vulgate: Docete me, et ego tacebo :  et si quid forte ignoravi, instruite me.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: ה֭וֹרוּנִי וַאֲנִ֣י אַחֲרִ֑ישׁ וּמַה־שָּׁ֝גִ֗יתִי הָבִ֥ינוּ לִֽי׃
verses.douay_rheims: Teach me, and I will hold my peace: and if I have been ignorant of any thing, instruct me.
verses.finnish_biblia: Opettakaat minua, minä olen ääneti, ja jota en minä tiedä, niin neuvokaat minua.
verses.king_james: Teach me, and I will hold my tongue: and cause me to understand wherein I have erred.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quare detraxistis sermonibus veritatis,  cum e vobis nullus sit qui possit arguere me ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: מַה־נִּמְרְצ֥וּ אִמְרֵי־יֹ֑שֶׁר וּמַה־יּוֹכִ֖יחַ הוֹכֵ֣חַ מִכֶּֽם׃
verses.douay_rheims: Why have you detracted the words of truth, whereas there is none of you that can reprove me?
verses.finnish_biblia: Kuinka vahvat ovat oikeuden puheet? kuka teissä on se, joka sitä laittaa taitaa?
verses.king_james: How forcible are right words! but what doth your arguing reprove?

verses.latin_vulgate: Ad increpandum tantum eloquia concinnatis,  et in ventum verba profertis.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הַלְהוֹכַ֣ח מִלִּ֣ים תַּחְשֹׁ֑בוּ וּ֝לְר֗וּחַ אִמְרֵ֥י נֹאָֽשׁ׃
verses.douay_rheims: You dress up speeches only to rebuke, and you utter words to the wind.
verses.finnish_biblia: Te ajattelette sanoja, ainoastaan nuhdellaksenne, ja teette sanoillanne epäileväisen mielen.
verses.king_james: Do ye imagine to reprove words, and the speeches of one that is desperate, which are as wind?

verses.latin_vulgate: Super pupillum irruitis,  et subvertere nitimini amicum vestrum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אַף־עַל־יָת֥וֹם תַּפִּ֑ילוּ וְ֝תִכְר֗וּ עַֽל־רֵיעֲכֶֽם׃
verses.douay_rheims: You rush in upon the fatherless, and you endeavour to overthrow your friend.
verses.finnish_biblia: Te karkaatte köyhän orvon päälle, ja kaivatte lähimmäisellenne kuoppaa.
verses.king_james: Yea, ye overwhelm the fatherless, and ye dig a pit for your friend.

verses.latin_vulgate: Verumtamen quod cœpistis explete :  præbete aurem, et videte an mentiar.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְ֭עַתָּה הוֹאִ֣ילוּ פְנוּ־בִ֑י וְעַל־פְּ֝נֵיכֶ֗ם אִם־אֲכַזֵּֽב׃
verses.douay_rheims: However, finish what you have begun: give ear and see whether I lie.
verses.finnish_biblia: Mutta että te nyt tahdotte, niin katsokaat minun päälleni, jos minä teidän edessänne valhettelen.
verses.king_james: Now therefore be content, look upon me; for it is evident unto you if I lie.

verses.latin_vulgate: Respondete, obsecro, absque contentione ;  et loquentes id quod justum est, judicate.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: שֽׁוּבוּ־נָ֭א אַל־תְּהִ֣י עַוְלָ֑ה (ושבי) [וְשׁ֥וּבוּ] ע֝֗וֹד צִדְקִי־בָֽהּ׃
verses.douay_rheims: Answer, I beseech you, without contention: and speaking that which is just, judge ye.
verses.finnish_biblia: Palatkaat nyt, olkoon pois vääryys: Tulkaat jälleen; minun vastaukseni pitää oikia oleman.
verses.king_james: Return, I pray you, let it not be iniquity; yea, return again, my righteousness is in it.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et non invenietis in lingua mea iniquitatem,  nec in faucibus meis stultitia personabit.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הֲיֵשׁ־בִּלְשׁוֹנִ֥י עַוְלָ֑ה אִם־חִ֝כִּ֗י לֹא־יָבִ֥ין הַוּֽוֹת׃
verses.douay_rheims: And you shall not find iniquity in my tongue, neither shall folly sound in my mouth.
verses.finnish_biblia: Onko minun kielessäni vääryyttä? eikö minun suulakeni ymmärrä vaivoja?
verses.king_james: Is there iniquity in my tongue? cannot my taste discern perverse things?
